title: Tricuspis pilosa
intro: Tricuspis pilosa, also known as Tricuspis tricuspis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Tricuspis pilosa is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Tricuspis pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Tricuspis pilosa is commonly prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used in the form of a powder or a tincture for treating various health conditions.
taxonomy: Tricuspis pilosa is classified under the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. It belongs to the order Poales and the class Liliopsida. This plant is further grouped within the subfamily Eriocauloideae. The genus Tricuspis is part of the family Eriocaulaceae and is native to Africa. The classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
